ev.headline: San Francisco Expands Curbside EV Charging Infrastructure by 2030
ev.summary: Mayor Daniel Lurie introduced legislation to expand curbside EV charging access across San Francisco to achieve the city's net-zero emissions goals by 2040, targeting 25% of new vehicles to be electric.

ev.headline: Mercedes-Benz VLE Electric Luxury Van Launches with 700 km Range and 800V Charging
ev.summary: Mercedes unveiled the all-electric VLE, an eight-seat grand limousine featuring 800-volt charging capability, over 700 km WLTP range, and flexible seating on the new Van Architecture platform.

ev.headline: Rivian R2 EV Launch Set as One of Fastest Ramp-ups in US History
ev.summary: TD Cowen upgrades Rivian to buy ahead of R2 launch, projecting 212,335k annual units and positioning the affordable compact EV to capitalize on rebounding US market demand.

ev.headline: Porsche Cayenne S Electric Launches at $126,300 with 657 HP and 405-Mile Range
ev.summary: Porsche introduced the Cayenne S Electric variant, priced $36,700 lower than the Turbo model, offering AWD performance with 657 hp and up to 405 miles WLTP range with deliveries beginning Summer 2026.

ev.headline: ION Storage Systems Achieves First US Solid-State Battery Qualification Milestone
ev.summary: Maryland-based ION Storage Systems announced its Cornerstone solid-state battery cells passed customer qualification, marking a major milestone as the first US company to achieve this for cell performance with production starting 2026.

ev.headline: Redmond Launches Free RedLink All-Electric Shuttle Service to Light Rail
ev.summary: Redmond, Washington launched RedLink, a free on-demand all-electric ride service to connect residents and visitors to the Sound Transit light rail system, partnering with transportation company Circuit.

ev.headline: Used EV Sales Surge 21% Year-Over-Year Despite Federal Tax Credit Elimination
ev.summary: Used EV sales in the US reached 31,503 units in January 2026, up 21% from a year earlier, as falling values and improved technology make second-hand electric vehicles more accessible than new cars.

ev.headline: DC Fast-Charging Infrastructure Grew 17.8% in 2025, Adding 2,200+ Locations
ev.summary: The US added nearly 17,000 DC fast-charging stalls in 2025, ending the year with 68,000 stalls, averaging six new locations online daily as infrastructure expands despite policy uncertainty.

ev.headline: Solid-State Battery Hits 80% Charge in 4.5 Minutes in Independent Test
ev.summary: Independent testing confirmed Donut Lab's solid-state battery reached 80% charge in under five minutes, addressing a key EV adoption barrier, though industry skepticism remains about commercial viability.

ev.headline: Electric Vehicle Sales Plummeted 20% in January 2026 vs December
ev.summary: US EV sales fell sharply in January compared to December as federal tax credits remain eliminated and industry faces challenges in finding natural market demand without policy support.

ev.headline: Ford and Major Automakers Pivot Strategy Toward Hybrids Amid EV Headwinds
ev.summary: Ford takes $19.5 billion charge to shift investment focus toward profitable hybrid and smaller EV models, while GM and other legacy automakers scale back EV production in favor of gas-powered trucks and SUVs.

ev.headline: BYD's EV Sales Dropped 36% in Early 2026 Despite Global Leadership Position
ev.summary: China's BYD, world's largest EV seller, saw a 36% sales decline in early 2026 as Chinese competitors gained market share and consumer demand diversified across multiple offerings.

ev.headline: Toyota Targeting 2027-2028 for Solid-State Battery Mass Production
ev.summary: Toyota, holding over 1,000 solid-state battery patents, aims to bring solid-state batteries to mass production by 2027-2028 and integrate them into vehicles by 2028 as a key competitive advantage.

ev.headline: Trump Administration Rolls Back Federal EV Fuel Economy Standards to 2022 Levels
ev.summary: NHTSA proposed rolling back fuel economy standards to 2022 requirements with minimal annual increases through 2031, achieving only 34.5 mpg fleet average versus 43.2 mpg in 2024.

ev.headline: Trump Administration Freezes $7.5B in EV Charging Infrastructure Funds Nationwide
ev.summary: Trump administration froze federal EV charging expansion funds with multiple states, including Wisconsin, now suing over the executive order halting progress on highway fast-charging corridors.

ev.headline: Subaru Unveils 2026 Trailseeker EV SUV with 375 HP and 300+ Mile Range
ev.summary: Subaru debuted the all-electric Trailseeker EV SUV at the 2025 New York Auto Show, featuring 375 horsepower, legendary all-wheel drive, and NACS charging compatibility across 15,000+ nationwide locations.

ev.headline: 43 New Electric Vehicles Expected to Debut in US Market During 2026
ev.summary: Industry research shows up to 43 new EV models could debut or reach US dealers in 2026, including launches from Acura, Porsche, Mercedes, and others despite ongoing market challenges and policy uncertainty.

ev.headline: Chinese Semi-Solid-State EV Battery Claims 620-Mile Range with 500 Wh/kg Density
ev.summary: A Chinese EV maker announced development of a semi-solid-state battery with 500+ wh/kg energy density—30% higher than lithium-ion—claiming potential for 1,000-plus mile range vehicles.

ev.headline: Volkswagen Targets 80% European EV Sales by 2030 with Aggressive NEW AUTO Strategy
ev.summary: Volkswagen aims for approximately 80% all-electric vehicle sales in Europe by 2030 through its NEW AUTO strategy, reducing carbon footprint 30% and investing heavily in modular EV platforms.

ev.headline: CaliforniaEV Drivers Secure 92% Satisfaction, Plan to Repurchase Electric Vehicles
ev.summary: Despite federal policy headwinds, 92% of current EV owners plan to buy another EV for their next vehicle, with used EV market expansion and high satisfaction driving continued adoption momentum.

ev.headline: All 50 US States Adopt Multiple EV-Supportive Policies Despite Federal Rollbacks
ev.summary: EDF report reveals all 50 states and DC have adopted minimum four EV policies each, with California leading with all 16 EV support policies as states accelerate clean transportation despite federal uncertainty.

ev.headline: Karma Automotive Plans First US Mass-Production Solid-State Battery Vehicle by Late 2026
ev.summary: Karma Automotive announced plans to deliver the Kaveya ultra-luxury coupe equipped with Factorial solid-state batteries, offering sub-3 second 0-60 times and advanced battery technology for the US market.

ev.headline: GM and Ford Reduce EV Mandates in Response to Changing Market Dynamics and Policy Shifts
ev.summary: General Motors and Ford are reverting to hybrid strategies and reducing pure EV commitments as consumer demand proves less robust than anticipated and federal support diminishes dramatically.

ev.headline: Federal Electric Vehicle Tax Credits Expire; Used EV Market Rapidly Expands to Fill Gap
ev.summary: With federal EV tax credits eliminated, used EV market is experiencing rapid growth as prices fall and become competitive with gas vehicles, supporting continued EV adoption among price-sensitive consumers.

ev.headline: Tesla Loses Market Share to Competitors as Global EV Competition Intensifies
ev.summary: Tesla's share of global battery-electric vehicle sales has declined from 20% to mid-teens as Chinese automakers, legacy OEMs, and new entrants rapidly expand EV offerings and compete on price.

energy.headline: California Appeals Court Upholds Reduced Solar Reimbursement Rates
energy.summary: A California appellate court affirmed the Public Utilities Commission decision to sharply cut compensation for homeowners and businesses exporting rooftop solar electricity to the grid.

energy.headline: Solar Panel Installations Drop 14% Amid Policy Rollbacks and Grid Demand Challenges
energy.summary: U.S. solar panel installations fell 14% as the Trump administration rolled back subsidies and incentives for renewable energy while managing growing grid demand.

energy.headline: U.S. Adds 43.2GW Solar Capacity in 2025, Down 14% from Prior Year
energy.summary: The U.S. installed 43.2GW of solar in 2025, a 14% decrease from 2024, though solar maintained 54% of all new electricity generation capacity added to the grid.

energy.headline: Batteries Becoming 'New Solar' as Net Metering Evolves and Energy Prices Soar
energy.summary: As net metering policies change and electricity prices climb, homeowners increasingly prioritize storing solar energy for personal use rather than exporting excess power to the grid.

energy.headline: Record 86 GW of U.S. Capacity Planned for 2026, with Solar and Storage Leading
energy.summary: A historic 86 GW of new U.S. generating capacity is slated for 2026, with solar and battery storage accounting for 79% of planned additions, marking record expansion.

energy.headline: Homeowners Rushed Solar Installations Before Tax Credit Expiry, Driving 205% Surge in Demand
energy.summary: U.S. homeowners surged to install solar before the 30% federal tax credit ended on December 31, 2025, driving a 205% increase in homeowner engagement with solar installers.

energy.headline: Energy Storage Deployment Surpasses 57 GWh in 2025, Up 29% Year-Over-Year
energy.summary: Battery energy storage installations reached record levels in 2025, surpassing 57 GWh with a 29% year-over-year increase, supporting grid reliability and renewable integration.

energy.headline: Colorado Advances Portable Solar and 'Meter Collar' Installation Technology
energy.summary: Colorado is clearing the way for portable plug-in solar panels and meter collars that reduce installation costs from $10,000 to $500-$700, enabling more affordable residential solar access.

energy.headline: California Residential Battery Attachment Reaches 69% as Storage Adoption Accelerates
energy.summary: California's residential battery attachment rate has climbed to 69%, reflecting rising adoption of solar-plus-storage systems in response to policy changes and grid reliability concerns.

energy.headline: Battery Storage Expected to Grow 21% in 2026 Despite Policy Headwinds
energy.summary: U.S. battery storage is projected to grow 21% in 2026 despite tariffs and shifting renewable energy policies, driven by strong economics and rising electricity demand.

energy.headline: US Residential Solar Enters Post-Incentive Era After Dramatic Year-End ITC Surge
energy.summary: As the 30% federal residential tax credit expired December 31, 2025, the U.S. residential solar market entered a new phase with battery attachment declining from 41% to 38% during the rush.

energy.headline: 58 GWh of Energy Storage Capacity Installed in 2025, 4x Growth from Three Years Ago
energy.summary: The U.S. installed a record-breaking 58 GWh of energy storage capacity in 2025, quadrupling deployment from three years prior, with solar and storage accounting for 84% of new grid capacity.

energy.headline: Arizona Shifts to Net Billing as Utilities Eliminate Full Retail Net Metering
energy.summary: Arizona utilities no longer offer traditional full retail net metering for new residential systems, instead operating under net billing frameworks where solar systems maximize on-site consumption.

energy.headline: Senate Legislation Supports Plug-In Solar DIY Systems Across Multiple States
energy.summary: Republican and Democratic lawmakers in more than half of U.S. states have introduced legislation boosting adoption of DIY solar systems including portable balcony solar panels.

energy.headline: Most U.S. Households Can Save Money and Weather Blackouts with Solar-Plus-Storage
energy.summary: A Stanford study found 60% of U.S. families could reduce electricity costs by 15% on average by installing a solar-battery system, while utility rate changes incentivize battery adoption.

energy.headline: Virtual Power Plants Dispatch 535 MW in California Test, Powering Half of San Francisco's Demand
energy.summary: Over 100,000 California homes participated in a virtual power plant test that successfully dispatched 535 megawatts, demonstrating the grid management potential of residential solar and storage.

energy.headline: Federal Tax Credit Elimination Prompts Shift Toward Third-Party Solar Ownership Models
energy.summary: With the residential tax credit ending December 31, 2025, solar market is shifting toward third-party ownership like leases and power purchase agreements which remain eligible for incentives through 2028.

energy.headline: California Launches $280 Million Program Expanding Solar and Battery Access for Low-Income Households
energy.summary: The California Public Utilities Commission launched a new statewide initiative to help low-income customers install battery storage and solar systems with full cost coverage when combined with federal credits.

energy.headline: Illinois Governor Signs 2025 Clean Grid Act Expanding Battery Storage and Virtual Power Plants
energy.summary: Illinois Governor J.B. Pritzker signed the 2025 Clean and Reliable Grid Affordability Act, advancing statewide battery storage expansion and virtual power plant programs.

energy.headline: Colorado Extends Residential Energy Storage Tax Credit Through 2030
energy.summary: Colorado extended its residential energy storage tax credit through 2030 and approved the state's first Virtual Power Plant program with fair compensation tariffs for distributed energy resources.

energy.headline: Virginia Utilities Push to Reduce Net Metering Rates from $0.16 to $0.04 per kWh
energy.summary: Dominion Energy and Appalachian Power are proposing significant cuts to Virginia net metering rates, threatening solar economics for systems installed in 2025 and beyond.

energy.headline: San Diego Expands Solar Battery Savings Program with Performance Incentives for Grid Support
energy.summary: San Diego Community Power expanded its Solar Battery Savings program offering upfront rebates and performance incentive payments when home batteries support the grid during peak demand hours.

energy.headline: Texas Passes Legislation Strengthening Rooftop Solar and Home Battery Storage Access
energy.summary: Texas rejected proposed restrictions on solar and storage while passing new laws strengthening homeowners' freedom to choose affordable rooftop solar and battery storage systems.

energy.headline: Average Lithium Home Battery Cost $7,000-$30,000 Installed in 2025
energy.summary: Lithium home storage battery systems averaged $1,300 per kWh after the 30% federal tax credit, with Tesla Powerwall 3 priced at $15,400-$16,500 before incentives.

energy.headline: Residential Solar Retrofit Battery Storage Market Emerges as Growth Opportunity
energy.summary: Millions of U.S. homes with solar-only systems installed between 2016-2023 now represent a major opportunity for battery storage retrofits as net metering policies shift.
